experiment: E-X4 (PREREG_FRENCH49.md addendum E, CPU emergent forgetting)

config.protocol: run_experiments.e1 conventions: schedule rng 1000+s, market seed 5000+s (same data per arm), arm rng 99*s+7; T from make_schedule

hyperparameters.architecture: trunk 20->32->32 (tanh) + linear heads on the 32-dim representation; trunk params = 20*32+32 + 32*32+32 = 1760; +33 per head; heads zero-initialised (matches linear Specialist w=0); trunk init N(0, 1/sqrt(fan_in)), identical across arms per seed (same rng seed 99*s+7, same draw order)
hyperparameters.loss: SPO+ decision surrogate (risp.spo_plus_loss_grad algebra), gradient w.r.t. y_hat backpropagated through head + trunk
hyperparameters.optimizer: plain SGD, fixed lr, no momentum/decay/clipping
hyperparameters.buffer: arm-level append-only regime->episode->days store; NOTHING ever deleted or capped (no buf_days/buf_episodes caps)

competition_A5_A6.window_winner_training: 1 step per window day at window close (mirrors risp.RISPArm._close_window)
competition_A5_A6.simplifications: N heads on one shared trunk instead of N capacity-K specialists (no capacity/eviction by design); single shared append-only buffer instead of per-specialist buffers; untrained head serves the zero-head output instead of 1e-6 tie-break noise. Competition dynamics, scoring, EG step, pin rule and constants unchanged from risp.RISPArm.

A6_invariance.objective: Var_e[lbar_e] + beta*E_e[lbar_e] over ALL stored episodes of the active regime (uncapped, vs linear's 6-episode cap)

hyperparameters.tuning: defaults picked in advance from the linear arms' constants (lr scaled 0.08->0.05 for the deeper credit path); one single-seed pilot run to confirm learning/runtime only; no hyperparameter search / no adjustment round used

gamma_mlp.definition: A1-mlp - A9-mlp post-react, paired per seed

verdict: Gamma-mlp > 0: paired 95% CI excludes 0 from above ([0.00931, 0.01106]). Forgetting EMERGES as gradient interference in the shared trunk.
pre_committed_interpretation: Gamma>0 emerges (~55%) -> "coded, not emergent" objection dies

pre_committed_interpretation_both_branch.gamma_positive: Gamma>0 emerges (~55%) -> "coded, not emergent" objection dies
pre_committed_interpretation_both_branch.gamma_zero: Gamma~=0 (~45%) -> the synthetic deficit is an artifact of the hard-memory model and the paper's synthetic headline takes a scope caveat at full prominence

expansion_precommit.snr_sweep_prediction: at 4x SNR the MLP monolith should match or beat the pinned arms (the inversion reproduces, ~60%); if instead retention keeps paying at high SNR under gradient descent, that's a genuine divergence from the linear result and must be reported at full prominence.
expansion_precommit.expansion_100seed_prediction: inherits the minimal-battery E-X4 branches unchanged (Gamma>0 emerges vs Gamma~=0); no new researcher degrees of freedom.
expansion_precommit.snr_mechanism: mirrors run_experiments.e6(): cfg = SynthConfig(snr_mult=snr); snr_mult scales theta_r and gamma_{r,e} (signal), noise untouched; same seed conventions (schedule 1000+s, market 5000+s, arm 99*s+7).
expansion_precommit.adjudication_rule_4x: primary metric post_react (Gamma convention), steady reported alongside. Let dA9 = paired per-seed A1-A9 and dA6 = paired A1-A6 post-react diffs at 4x. If either paired 95% CI has lower bound > 0 (a pinned arm still significantly beats the monolith) -> retention keeps paying -> DIVERGENCE at full prominence. Else if both CI upper bounds < 0 -> monolith BEATS the pinned arms (full inversion). Else -> monolith MATCHES the pinned arms (inversion reproduces in match form).

expansion_100seed.protocol: identical to the minimal battery (see config block); seeds 0..99 under the same conventions

gamma_mlp.definition: A1-mlp - A9-mlp post_react, paired per seed

gamma_mlp_steady.definition: A1-mlp - A9-mlp steady, paired per seed

expansion_100seed.verdict: Gamma-mlp > 0 at 100 seeds: paired 95% CI excludes 0 from above ([0.00973, 0.01046]).
expansion_100seed.pre_committed_interpretation: Gamma>0 emerges (~55%) -> "coded, not emergent" objection dies

snr_sweep.snr_mechanism: mirrors run_experiments.e6(): cfg = SynthConfig(snr_mult=snr); snr_mult scales theta_r and gamma_{r,e} (signal), noise untouched; same seed conventions (schedule 1000+s, market 5000+s, arm 99*s+7).
snr_sweep.prediction: at 4x SNR the MLP monolith should match or beat the pinned arms (the inversion reproduces, ~60%); if instead retention keeps paying at high SNR under gradient descent, that's a genuine divergence from the linear result and must be reported at full prominence.
snr_sweep.adjudication_rule_4x: primary metric post_react (Gamma convention), steady reported alongside. Let dA9 = paired per-seed A1-A9 and dA6 = paired A1-A6 post-react diffs at 4x. If either paired 95% CI has lower bound > 0 (a pinned arm still significantly beats the monolith) -> retention keeps paying -> DIVERGENCE at full prominence. Else if both CI upper bounds < 0 -> monolith BEATS the pinned arms (full inversion). Else -> monolith MATCHES the pinned arms (inversion reproduces in match form).

snr_sweep.verdict_4x: DIVERGENCE (full prominence): retention keeps paying at 4x SNR under gradient descent -- a pinned arm still significantly beats the monolith on post-react (A1-A9 CI [0.05164, 0.05791]; A1-A6 CI [0.05083, 0.05738]). This diverges from the linear E6 inversion.
snr_sweep.post_hoc_note: POST-HOC (written after adjudication, clearly labeled as such, no bearing on the pre-committed verdict): the divergence has a plausible architectural reading. Linear E6's A1-monolith holds K=2 regime-keyed heads (it can re-fit a switched-to regime into a fresh head within days at high SNR), whereas the E-X4 monolith is, by binding prereg design, ONE trunk + ONE head with no regime conditioning: every switch must overwrite the single readout, and higher SNR scales the signal being overwritten, so interference grows with SNR instead of washing out. The 4x comparison is therefore not apples-to-apples with linear E6's A1; the divergence is real but may reflect the single-readout constraint rather than a failure of the fresh-learner break-even mechanism under SGD. A9/A5/A6 remain matched to their linear counterparts in role.
